content.thinking: Interesting - PARSER_DELIMITER and PARSER_PUNCTUATION are not found in the language adapters. Let me look at the semantic_types.hpp to understand the actual constant values and see how semantic types are mapped in the adapters.

content.content:
{"result":"     1	#pragma once
     2	
     3	#include "duckdb.hpp"
     4	
     5	namespace duckdb {
     6	
     7	// 8-bit semantic type encoding:
     8	// Byte layout: [ ss kk tt ll ]
     9	// ss = super_kind (bits 6-7): META_EXTERNAL=00, DATA_STRUCTURE=01, CONTROL_EFFECTS=10, COMPUTATION=11
    10	// kk = kind (bits 4-5): 4 kinds within each super_kind
    11	// tt = super_type (bits 2-3): 4 variants within each kind
    12	// ll = language_specific (bits 0-1): language-specific sub-type (unused for now)
    13	
    14	namespace SemanticTypes {
    15	
    16	// Super kinds (bits 6-7)
    17	constexpr uint8_t META_EXTERNAL = 0x00;   // 00xx xxxx
    18	constexpr uint8_t DATA_STRUCTURE = 0x40;  // 01xx xxxx
    19	constexpr uint8_t CONTROL_EFFECTS = 0x80; // 10xx xxxx
    20	constexpr uint8_t COMPUTATION = 0xC0;     // 11xx xxxx
    21	
    22	// Kinds within META_EXTERNAL (00ss ssxx)
    23	constexpr uint8_t PARSER_SPECIFIC = META_EXTERNAL | 0x00; // 0000 xxxx
    24	constexpr uint8_t RESERVED = META_EXTERNAL | 0x10;        // 0001 xxxx
    25	constexpr uint8_t METADATA = META_EXTERNAL | 0x20;        // 0010 xxxx
    26	constexpr uint8_t EXTERNAL = META_EXTERNAL | 0x30;        // 0011 xxxx
    27	
    28	// Kinds within DATA_STRUCTURE (01ss ssxx)
    29	constexpr uint8_t LITERAL = DATA_STRUCTURE | 0x00; // 0100 xxxx
    30	constexpr uint8_t NAME = DATA_STRUCTURE | 0x10;    // 0101 xxxx
    31	constexpr uint8_t PATTERN = DATA_STRUCTURE | 0x20; // 0110 xxxx
    32	constexpr uint8_t TYPE = DATA_STRUCTURE | 0x30;    // 0111 xxxx
    33	
    34	// Kinds within COMPUTATION (11ss ssxx)
    35	constexpr uint8_t OPERATOR = COMPUTATION | 0x00;         // 1100 xxxx
    36	constexpr uint8_t COMPUTATION_NODE = COMPUTATION | 0x10; // 1101 xxxx
    37	constexpr uint8_t TRANSFORM = COMPUTATION | 0x20;        // 1110 xxxx
    38	constexpr uint8_t DEFINITION = COMPUTATION | 0x30;       // 1111 xxxx
    39	
    40	// Kinds within CONTROL_EFFECTS (10ss ssxx)
    41	constexpr uint8_t EXECUTION = CONTROL_EFFECTS | 0x00;      // 1000 xxxx
    42	constexpr uint8_t FLOW_CONTROL = CONTROL_EFFECTS | 0x10;   // 1001 xxxx
    43	constexpr uint8_t ERROR_HANDLING = CONTROL_EFFECTS | 0x20; // 1010 xxxx
    44	constexpr uint8_t ORGANIZATION = CONTROL_EFFECTS | 0x30;   // 1011 xxxx
    45	
    46	// ===== PARSER_SPECIFIC super types (0000 ttxx) =====
    47	constexpr uint8_t PARSER_CONSTRUCT = PARSER_SPECIFIC | 0x00;   // 0000 0000 - unique language constructs
    48	constexpr uint8_t PARSER_DELIMITER = PARSER_SPECIFIC | 0x04;   // 0000 0100 - delimiters, separators
    49	constexpr uint8_t PARSER_PUNCTUATION = PARSER_SPECIFIC | 0x08; // 0000 1000 - language-specific punctuation
    50	constexpr uint8_t PARSER_SYNTAX = PARSER_SPECIFIC | 0x0C;      // 0000 1100 - syntax elements
    51	
    52	// ===== RESERVED super types (0001 ttxx) =====
    53	constexpr uint8_t RESERVED_FUTURE1 = RESERVED | 0x00; // 0001 0000 - reserved for future use
    54	constexpr uint8_t RESERVED_FUTURE2 = RESERVED | 0x04; // 0001 0100 - reserved for future use
    55	constexpr uint8_t RESERVED_FUTURE3 = RESERVED | 0x08; // 0001 1000 - reserved for future use
    56	constexpr uint8_t RESERVED_FUTURE4 = RESERVED | 0x0C; // 0001 1100 - reserved for future use
    57	
    58	// ===== METADATA super types (0010 ttxx) =====
    59	constexpr uint8_t METADATA_COMMENT = METADATA | 0x00;    // 0010 0000 - comments, documentation
    60	constexpr uint8_t METADATA_ANNOTATION = METADATA | 0x04; // 0010 0100 - decorators, attributes
    61	constexpr uint8_t METADATA_DIRECTIVE = METADATA | 0x08;  // 0010 1000 - preprocessor directives
    62	constexpr uint8_t METADATA_DEBUG = METADATA | 0x0C;      // 0010 1100 - debug information, source maps
    63	
    64	// ===== EXTERNAL super types (0011 ttxx) =====
    65	constexpr uint8_t EXTERNAL_IMPORT = EXTERNAL | 0x00;  // 0011 0000 - import statements
    66	constexpr uint8_t EXTERNAL_EXPORT = EXTERNAL | 0x04;  // 0011 0100 - export statements
    67	constexpr uint8_t EXTERNAL_FOREIGN = EXTERNAL | 0x08; // 0011 1000 - foreign function interface
    68	constexpr uint8_t EXTERNAL_EMBED = EXTERNAL | 0x0C;   // 0011 1100 - embedded content (HTML, CSS, SQL)
    69	
    70	// ===== LITERAL super types (0100 ttxx) =====
    71	constexpr uint8_t LITERAL_NUMBER = LITERAL | 0x00;     // 0100 0000 - integers, floats, decimals
    72	constexpr uint8_t LITERAL_STRING = LITERAL | 0x04;     // 0100 0100 - strings, chars, text
    73	constexpr uint8_t LITERAL_ATOMIC = LITERAL | 0x08;     // 0100 1000 - true, false, null, None, undefined
    74	constexpr uint8_t LITERAL_STRUCTURED = LITERAL | 0x0C; // 0100 1100 - arrays, objects, composite
    75	
    76	// ===== NAME super types (0101 ttxx) =====
    77	constexpr uint8_t NAME_IDENTIFIER = NAME | 0x00; // 0101 0000 - simple identifiers
    78	constexpr uint8_t NAME_QUALIFIED = NAME | 0x04;  // 0101 0100 - qualified names (obj.prop)
    79	constexpr uint8_t NAME_SCOPED = NAME | 0x08;     // 0101 1000 - scoped references (::, this, super, @instance_var)
    80	constexpr uint8_t NAME_ATTRIBUTE = NAME | 0x0C;  // 0101 1100 - decorative metadata (@decorator, @media, attributes)
    81	
    82	// ===== PATTERN super types (0110 ttxx) =====
    83	constexpr uint8_t PATTERN_DESTRUCTURE = PATTERN | 0x00; // 0110 0000 - breaking apart into multiple terms
    84	constexpr uint8_t PATTERN_COLLECT = PATTERN | 0x04;     // 0110 0100 - gathering together into single term
    85	constexpr uint8_t PATTERN_TEMPLATE = PATTERN | 0x08;    // 0110 1000 - parameterized patterns
    86	constexpr uint8_t PATTERN_MATCH = PATTERN | 0x0C;       // 0110 1100 - structured entities for comparison
    87	
    88	// ===== TYPE super types (0111 ttxx) =====
    89	constexpr uint8_t TYPE_PRIMITIVE = TYPE | 0x00; // 0111 0000 - basic types (int, string, etc)
    90	constexpr uint8_t TYPE_COMPOSITE = TYPE | 0x04; // 0111 0100 - structs, unions, tuples
    91	constexpr uint8_t TYPE_REFERENCE = TYPE | 0x08; // 0111 1000 - pointers, references
    92	constexpr uint8_t TYPE_GENERIC = TYPE | 0x0C;   // 0111 1100 - generic/template types
    93	
    94	// ===== OPERATOR super types (1100 ttxx) =====
    95	constexpr uint8_t OPERATOR_ARITHMETIC = OPERATOR | 0x00; // 1100 0000 - +, -, *, /, %, **, //, &, |, ^, ~, <<, >>
    96	constexpr uint8_t OPERATOR_LOGICAL = OPERATOR | 0x04;    // 1100 0100 - &&, ||, !, and, or, not, ? :
    97	constexpr uint8_t OPERATOR_COMPARISON = OPERATOR | 0x08; // 1100 1000 - ==, !=, <, >, <=, >=, ===, is, in, not in
    98	constexpr uint8_t OPERATOR_ASSIGNMENT = OPERATOR | 0x0C; // 1100 1100 - =, +=, -=, *=, /=, :=, etc.
    99	
   100	// ===== COMPUTATION_NODE super types (1101 ttxx) =====
   101	constexpr uint8_t COMPUTATION_CALL = COMPUTATION_NODE | 0x00;       // 1101 0000 - function calls
   102	constexpr uint8_t COMPUTATION_ACCESS = COMPUTATION_NODE | 0x04;     // 1101 0100 - member access, indexing
   103	constexpr uint8_t COMPUTATION_EXPRESSION = COMPUTATION_NODE | 0x08; // 1101 1000 - complex expressions
   104	constexpr uint8_t COMPUTATION_CLOSURE = COMPUTATION_NODE | 0x0C;    // 1101 1100 - closures, captured environments
   105	
   106	// ===== TRANSFORM super types (1110 ttxx) =====
   107	constexpr uint8_t TRANSFORM_QUERY = TRANSFORM | 0x00;       // 1110 0000 - SQL queries, LINQ
   108	constexpr uint8_t TRANSFORM_ITERATION = TRANSFORM | 0x04;   // 1110 0100 - map, filter, reduce
   109	constexpr uint8_t TRANSFORM_PROJECTION = TRANSFORM | 0x08;  // 1110 1000 - select, extract operations
   110	constexpr uint8_t TRANSFORM_AGGREGATION = TRANSFORM | 0x0C; // 1110 1100 - group by, aggregate ops
   111	
   112	// ===== DEFINITION super types (1111 ttxx) =====
   113	constexpr uint8_t DEFINITION_FUNCTION = DEFINITION | 0x00; // 1111 0000 - function definitions
   114	constexpr uint8_t DEFINITION_VARIABLE = DEFINITION | 0x04; // 1111 0100 - variable/constant definitions
   115	constexpr uint8_t DEFINITION_CLASS = DEFINITION | 0x08;    // 1111 1000 - class/struct definitions
   116	constexpr uint8_t DEFINITION_MODULE = DEFINITION | 0x0C;   // 1111 1100 - modules, namespaces
   117	
   118	// ===== EXECUTION super types (1000 ttxx) =====
   119	constexpr uint8_t EXECUTION_STATEMENT = EXECUTION | 0x00;      // 1000 0000 - expression statements
   120	constexpr uint8_t EXECUTION_DECLARATION = EXECUTION | 0x04;    // 1000 0100 - variable declarations
   121	constexpr uint8_t EXECUTION_STATEMENT_CALL = EXECUTION | 0x08; // 1000 1000 - statement-level invocations
   122	constexpr uint8_t EXECUTION_MUTATION = EXECUTION | 0x0C;       // 1000 1100 - assignments, scope modifications
   123	
   124	// ===== FLOW_CONTROL super types (1001 ttxx) =====
   125	constexpr uint8_t FLOW_CONDITIONAL = FLOW_CONTROL | 0x00; // 1001 0000 - if, switch, match
   126	constexpr uint8_t FLOW_LOOP = FLOW_CONTROL | 0x04;        // 1001 0100 - for, while, do-while
   127	constexpr uint8_t FLOW_JUMP = FLOW_CONTROL | 0x08;        // 1001 1000 - break, continue, return, goto
   128	constexpr uint8_t FLOW_SYNC = FLOW_CONTROL | 0x0C;        // 1001 1100 - async, await, synchronized, yield
   129	
   130	// ===== ERROR_HANDLING super types (1010 ttxx) =====
   131	constexpr uint8_t ERROR_TRY = ERROR_HANDLING | 0x00;     // 1010 0000 - try blocks
   132	constexpr uint8_t ERROR_CATCH = ERROR_HANDLING | 0x04;   // 1010 0100 - catch, except blocks
   133	constexpr uint8_t ERROR_THROW = ERROR_HANDLING | 0x08;   // 1010 1000 - throw, raise statements
   134	constexpr uint8_t ERROR_FINALLY = ERROR_HANDLING | 0x0C; // 1010 1100 - finally, ensure blocks
   135	
   136	// ===== ORGANIZATION super types (1011 ttxx) =====
   137	constexpr uint8_t ORGANIZATION_BLOCK = ORGANIZATION | 0x00;     // 1011 0000 - code blocks, scopes
   138	constexpr uint8_t ORGANIZATION_LIST = ORGANIZATION | 0x04;      // 1011 0100 - argument lists, parameter lists
   139	constexpr uint8_t ORGANIZATION_SECTION = ORGANIZATION | 0x08;   // 1011 1000 - sections, regions
   140	constexpr uint8_t ORGANIZATION_CONTAINER = ORGANIZATION | 0x0C; // 1011 1100 - files, modules, packages

   101	constexpr uint8_t COMPUTATION_CALL = COMPUTATION_NODE | 0x00;       // 1101 0000 - function calls
   102	constexpr uint8_t COMPUTATION_ACCESS = COMPUTATION_NODE | 0x04;     // 1101 0100 - member access, indexing
   103	constexpr uint8_t COMPUTATION_EXPRESSION = COMPUTATION_NODE | 0x08; // 1101 1000 - complex expressions
   104	constexpr uint8_t COMPUTATION_CLOSURE = COMPUTATION_NODE | 0x0C;    // 1101 1100 - closures, captured environments
   105	
   106	// ===== TRANSFORM super types (1110 ttxx) =====
   107	constexpr uint8_t TRANSFORM_QUERY = TRANSFORM | 0x00;       // 1110 0000 - SQL queries, LINQ
   108	constexpr uint8_t TRANSFORM_ITERATION = TRANSFORM | 0x04;   // 1110 0100 - map, filter, reduce
   109	constexpr uint8_t TRANSFORM_PROJECTION = TRANSFORM | 0x08;  // 1110 1000 - select, extract operations
   110	constexpr uint8_t TRANSFORM_AGGREGATION = TRANSFORM | 0x0C; // 1110 1100 - group by, aggregate ops
   111	
   112	// ===== DEFINITION super types (1111 ttxx) =====
   113	constexpr uint8_t DEFINITION_FUNCTION = DEFINITION | 0x00; // 1111 0000 - function definitions
   114	constexpr uint8_t DEFINITION_VARIABLE = DEFINITION | 0x04; // 1111 0100 - variable/constant definitions
   115	constexpr uint8_t DEFINITION_CLASS = DEFINITION | 0x08;    // 1111 1000 - class/struct definitions
   116	constexpr uint8_t DEFINITION_MODULE = DEFINITION | 0x0C;   // 1111 1100 - modules, namespaces
   117	
   118	// ===== EXECUTION super types (1000 ttxx) =====
   119	constexpr uint8_t EXECUTION_STATEMENT = EXECUTION | 0x00;      // 1000 0000 - expression statements
   120	constexpr uint8_t EXECUTION_DECLARATION = EXECUTION | 0x04;    // 1000 0100 - variable declarations
   121	constexpr uint8_t EXECUTION_STATEMENT_CALL = EXECUTION | 0x08; // 1000 1000 - statement-level invocations
   122	constexpr uint8_t EXECUTION_MUTATION = EXECUTION | 0x0C;       // 1000 1100 - assignments, scope modifications
   123	
   124	// ===== FLOW_CONTROL super types (1001 ttxx) =====
   125	constexpr uint8_t FLOW_CONDITIONAL = FLOW_CONTROL | 0x00; // 1001 0000 - if, switch, match
   126	constexpr uint8_t FLOW_LOOP = FLOW_CONTROL | 0x04;        // 1001 0100 - for, while, do-while
   127	constexpr uint8_t FLOW_JUMP = FLOW_CONTROL | 0x08;        // 1001 1000 - break, continue, return, goto
   128	constexpr uint8_t FLOW_SYNC = FLOW_CONTROL | 0x0C;        // 1001 1100 - async, await, synchronized, yield
   129	
   130	// ===== ERROR_HANDLING super types (1010 ttxx) =====
   131	constexpr uint8_t ERROR_TRY = ERROR_HANDLING | 0x00;     // 1010 0000 - try blocks
   132	constexpr uint8_t ERROR_CATCH = ERROR_HANDLING | 0x04;   // 1010 0100 - catch, except blocks
   133	constexpr uint8_t ERROR_THROW = ERROR_HANDLING | 0x08;   // 1010 1000 - throw, raise statements
   134	constexpr uint8_t ERROR_FINALLY = ERROR_HANDLING | 0x0C; // 1010 1100 - finally, ensure blocks
   135	
   136	// ===== ORGANIZATION super types (1011 ttxx) =====
   137	constexpr uint8_t ORGANIZATION_BLOCK = ORGANIZATION | 0x00;     // 1011 0000 - code blocks, scopes
   138	constexpr uint8_t ORGANIZATION_LIST = ORGANIZATION | 0x04;      // 1011 0100 - argument lists, parameter lists
   139	constexpr uint8_t ORGANIZATION_SECTION = ORGANIZATION | 0x08;   // 1011 1000 - sections, regions
   140	constexpr uint8_t ORGANIZATION_CONTAINER = ORGANIZATION | 0x0C; // 1011 1100 - files, modules, packages
   141	
   142	// Utility functions to extract components
   143	constexpr uint8_t GetSuperKind(uint8_t semantic_type) {
   144		return semantic_type & 0xC0; // Extract bits 6-7
   145	}
   146	
   147	constexpr uint8_t GetKind(uint8_t semantic_type) {
   148		return semantic_type & 0xF0; // Extract bits 4-7 (full kind value)
   149	}
   150	
   151	constexpr uint8_t GetSuperType(uint8_t semantic_type) {
   152		return (semantic_type & 0x0C) >> 2; // Extract bits 2-3 and shift to 0-3
   153	}
   154	
   155	constexpr uint8_t GetLanguageSpecific(uint8_t semantic_type) {
   156		return semantic_type & 0x03; // Extract bits 0-1
   157	}
   158	
   159	// Get human-readable names
   160	string GetSemanticTypeName(uint8_t semantic_type);
   161	string GetSuperKindName(uint8_t super_kind);
   162	string GetKindName(uint8_t kind);
   163	
   164	// Shorter convenience functions
   165	inline string TypeName(uint8_t code) {
   166		return GetSemanticTypeName(code);
   167	}
   168	inline string KindName(uint8_t kind) {
   169		return GetKindName(kind);
   170	}
   171	
   172	// Reverse lookups - name to code
   173	uint8_t GetSemanticTypeCode(const string &name);
   174	uint8_t GetKindCode(const string &name);
   175	uint8_t GetSuperKindCode(const string &name);
   176	
   177	// Shorter convenience functions
   178	inline uint8_t TypeCode(const string &name) {
   179		return GetSemanticTypeCode(name);
   180	}
   181	inline uint8_t KindCode(const string &name) {
   182		return GetKindCode(name);
   183	}
   184	
   185	// Helper predicates for common queries
   186	bool IsDefinition(uint8_t semantic_type);
   187	bool IsCall(uint8_t semantic_type);
   188	bool IsControlFlow(uint8_t semantic_type);
   189	bool IsIdentifier(uint8_t semantic_type);
   190	bool IsLiteral(uint8_t semantic_type);
   191	bool IsOperator(uint8_t semantic_type);
   192	bool IsType(uint8_t semantic_type);
   193	bool IsExternal(uint8_t semantic_type);
   194	bool IsError(uint8_t semantic_type);
   195	bool IsMetadata(uint8_t semantic_type);
   196	bool IsParserSpecific(uint8_t semantic_type);
   197	bool IsPunctuation(uint8_t semantic_type);
   198	
   199	// Get all types in a category
   200	vector<uint8_t> GetDefinitionTypes();
   201	vector<uint8_t> GetControlFlowTypes();
   202	vector<uint8_t> GetSearchableTypes(); // Types typically used in searches
   203	
   204	} // namespace SemanticTypes
   205	
   206	} // namespace duckdb

{"result":"     1	#pragma once
     2	
     3	#include "duckdb.hpp"
     4	#include "semantic_types.hpp"
     5	#include <unordered_map>
     6	
     7	namespace duckdb {
     8	
     9	// Extraction strategy for node names/values
    10	enum class ExtractionStrategy : uint8_t {
    11		NONE = 0,                      // No extraction needed
    12		NODE_TEXT = 1,                 // Extract the node's own text content
    13		FIRST_CHILD = 2,               // Extract text from first child
    14		FIND_IDENTIFIER = 3,           // Find first child of type "identifier"
    15		FIND_PROPERTY = 4,             // Find first child of type "property_identifier"
    16		FIND_ASSIGNMENT_TARGET = 5,    // Find identifier in parent assignment (universal pattern)
    17		FIND_QUALIFIED_IDENTIFIER = 6, // Find qualified/scoped identifiers and extract just the name part
    18		FIND_IN_DECLARATOR = 7,        // Find identifiers inside declarator nodes (universal pattern)
    19		FIND_CALL_TARGET = 8,          // Find method/function name from call (handles obj.method patterns)
    20		CUSTOM = 9                     // Language-specific custom logic
    21	};
    22	
    23	// Native context extraction strategies - pattern-based approach
    24	enum class NativeExtractionStrategy : uint8_t {
    25		NONE = 0,                 // No native context extraction
    26		FUNCTION_WITH_PARAMS,     // Standard function with parameter list
    27		FUNCTION_WITH_DECORATORS, // Function with annotations/decorators
    28		ARROW_FUNCTION,           // Lambda/arrow function pattern
    29		ASYNC_FUNCTION,           // Async function pattern
    30		CLASS_WITH_INHERITANCE,   // Class with base classes
    31		CLASS_WITH_METHODS,       // Class with method definitions
    32		VARIABLE_WITH_TYPE,       // Typed variable assignment
    33		GENERIC_FUNCTION,         // Function with generic parameters
    34		METHOD_DEFINITION,        // Class/object method
    35		CONSTRUCTOR_DEFINITION,   // Constructor/initializer
    36		INTERFACE_DEFINITION,     // Interface/trait/protocol
    37		ENUM_DEFINITION,          // Enum/union type
    38		IMPORT_STATEMENT,         // Import/include/using
    39		EXPORT_STATEMENT,         // Export/public declarations
    40		FUNCTION_CALL,            // Function call/invocation with arguments
    41		CUSTOM = 255              // Language-specific custom logic
    42	};
    43	
    44	// Simple node configuration
    45	struct NodeConfig {
    46		uint8_t semantic_type;                    // 8-bit: bits 2-7 = type, bits 0-1 = refinement
    47		ExtractionStrategy name_strategy;         // How to extract names
    48		NativeExtractionStrategy native_strategy; // Native context extraction pattern (repurposed from value_strategy)
    49		uint8_t flags;                            // Basic flags (IS_KEYWORD, IS_PUBLIC, IS_UNSAFE)
    50	
    51		// Constructor
    52		NodeConfig(uint8_t sem_type = 0, ExtractionStrategy name_strat = ExtractionStrategy::NONE,
    53		           NativeExtractionStrategy native_strat = NativeExtractionStrategy::NONE, uint8_t node_flags = 0)
    54		    : semantic_type(sem_type), name_strategy(name_strat), native_strategy(native_strat), flags(node_flags) {
    55		}
    56	};
    57	
    58	// Universal flags for orthogonal node properties
    59	// Design principle: Mark EXCEPTIONS, not the common case
    60	namespace ASTNodeFlags {
    61	constexpr uint8_t IS_SYNTAX_ONLY = 0x01;      // Pure syntax token (keyword, punctuation) - not a semantic construct
    62	constexpr uint8_t IS_DECLARATION_ONLY = 0x02; // Forward declaration without body (exception case)
    63	// 0x04 - 0x80: Reserved for future use
    64	
    65	// Backward compatibility: These are now no-ops (0x00)
    66	// Default assumptions:
    67	//   - Nodes ARE semantic constructs (not syntax-only)
    68	//   - Definitions HAVE bodies (not declaration-only)
    69	constexpr uint8_t IS_CONSTRUCT = 0x00; // DEPRECATED: no-op, semantic construct is now the default
    70	constexpr uint8_t IS_EMBODIED = 0x00;  // DEPRECATED: no-op, having a body is now the default
    71	
    72	// Keywords and punctuation are syntax-only tokens
    73	constexpr uint8_t IS_KEYWORD = IS_SYNTAX_ONLY;
    74	constexpr uint8_t IS_KEYWORD_IF_LEAF = IS_SYNTAX_ONLY;
    75	} // namespace ASTNodeFlags
    76	
    77	// Semantic refinement constants for fine-grained classification
    78	namespace SemanticRefinements {
    79	// DEFINITION_FUNCTION refinements (bits 0-1)
    80	namespace Function {
    81	constexpr uint8_t REGULAR = 0x00;     // 00 - Named functions, methods, procedures
    82	constexpr uint8_t LAMBDA = 0x01;      // 01 - Anonymous functions, closures, arrows
    83	constexpr uint8_t CONSTRUCTOR = 0x02; // 10 - Constructors, initializers, destructors
    84	constexpr uint8_t ASYNC = 0x03;       // 11 - Async, generator, coroutine functions
    85	} // namespace Function
    86	
    87	// LITERAL_NUMBER refinements
    88	namespace Number {
    89	constexpr uint8_t INTEGER = 0x00;    // 00 - All integer formats (decimal, hex, binary)
    90	constexpr uint8_t FLOAT = 0x01;      // 01 - Floating point numbers
    91	constexpr uint8_t SCIENTIFIC = 0x02; // 10 - Scientific notation (1.23e-4, 2E+5)
    92	constexpr uint8_t COMPLEX = 0x03;    // 11 - Complex numbers, rationals
    93	} // namespace Number
    94	
    95	// LITERAL_STRUCTURED refinements
    96	namespace Structured {
    97	constexpr uint8_t GENERIC = 0;  // 00 - Unspecified structured literals
    98	constexpr uint8_t SEQUENCE = 1; // 01 - Arrays, lists, tuples, vectors
    99	constexpr uint8_t MAPPING = 2;  // 10 - Objects, dictionaries, hashmaps
   100	constexpr uint8_t SET = 3;      // 11 - Sets, collections, unique containers
   101	} // namespace Structured
   102	
   103	// OPERATOR_ARITHMETIC refinements
   104	namespace Arithmetic {
   105	constexpr uint8_t BINARY = 0x00;  // 00 - +, -, *, /, %, standard binary ops
   106	constexpr uint8_t UNARY = 0x01;   // 01 - ++, --, unary +/-, sizeof
   107	constexpr uint8_t BITWISE = 0x02; // 10 - &, |, ^, <<, >>, bitwise operations
   108	constexpr uint8_t RANGE = 0x03;   // 11 - .., ..=, range and interval operators
   109	} // namespace Arithmetic
   110	
   111	// FLOW_CONDITIONAL refinements
   112	namespace Conditional {
   113	constexpr uint8_t BINARY = 0;   // 00 - if/else, unless, binary decisions
   114	constexpr uint8_t MULTIWAY = 1; // 01 - switch/case, match/when, pattern matching
   115	constexpr uint8_t GUARD = 2;    // 10 - guard statements, assertions, preconditions
   116	constexpr uint8_t TERNARY = 3;  // 11 - ?: operators, conditional expressions
   117	} // namespace Conditional
   118	
   119	// FLOW_LOOP refinements
   120	namespace Loop {
   121	constexpr uint8_t COUNTER = 0;     // 00 - for(int i=0; i<n; i++), counting loops
   122	constexpr uint8_t ITERATOR = 1;    // 01 - for-in, for-of, foreach, iterator-based
   123	constexpr uint8_t CONDITIONAL = 2; // 10 - while, until, condition-based loops
   124	constexpr uint8_t INFINITE = 3;    // 11 - loop, repeat, infinite loop constructs
   125	} // namespace Loop
   126	
   127	// ORGANIZATION refinements
   128	namespace Organization {
   129	constexpr uint8_t SEQUENTIAL = 0;   // 00 - Code blocks, statement sequences
   130	constexpr uint8_t COLLECTION = 1;   // 01 - Parameter lists, argument lists
   131	constexpr uint8_t MAPPING = 2;      // 10 - Named containers, objects
   132	constexpr uint8_t HIERARCHICAL = 3; // 11 - Modules, namespaces, packages
   133	} // namespace Organization
   134	
   135	// DEFINITION_VARIABLE refinements
   136	namespace Variable {
   137	constexpr uint8_t MUTABLE = 0x00;   // 00 - var, let, mutable variables
   138	constexpr uint8_t IMMUTABLE = 0x01; // 01 - const, final, readonly
   139	constexpr uint8_t PARAMETER = 0x02; // 10 - Function/method parameters
   140	constexpr uint8_t FIELD = 0x03;     // 11 - Class/struct fields, properties
   141	} // namespace Variable
   142	
   143	// COMPUTATION_CALL refinements
   144	namespace Call {
   145	constexpr uint8_t FUNCTION = 0x00;    // 00 - Regular function calls
   146	constexpr uint8_t METHOD = 0x01;      // 01 - Object method calls
   147	constexpr uint8_t CONSTRUCTOR = 0x02; // 10 - new ClassName(), constructors
   148	constexpr uint8_t MACRO = 0x03;       // 11 - Preprocessor macros, compile-time
   149	} // namespace Call
   150	
   151	// EXTERNAL_IMPORT refinements
   152	namespace Import {
   153	constexpr uint8_t MODULE = 0x00;    // 00 - import module, #include
   154	constexpr uint8_t SELECTIVE = 0x01; // 01 - from module import specific
   155	constexpr uint8_t WILDCARD = 0x02;  // 10 - import *, using namespace
   156	constexpr uint8_t RELATIVE = 0x03;  // 11 - from . import, relative imports
   157	} // namespace Import
   158	
   159	// LITERAL_STRING refinements
   160	namespace String {
   161	constexpr uint8_t LITERAL = 0x00;  // 00 - Basic quoted strings
   162	constexpr uint8_t TEMPLATE = 0x01; // 01 - Template strings, f-strings, interpolation
   163	constexpr uint8_t REGEX = 0x02;    // 10 - Regular expressions
   164	constexpr uint8_t RAW = 0x03;      // 11 - Raw strings, here-docs, verbatim
   165	} // namespace String
   166	
   167	// OPERATOR_COMPARISON refinements
   168	namespace Comparison {
   169	constexpr uint8_t EQUALITY = 0x00;   // 00 - ==, ===, !=, !==
   170	constexpr uint8_t RELATIONAL = 0x01; // 01 - <, >, <=, >=
   171	constexpr uint8_t MEMBERSHIP = 0x02; // 10 - in, instanceof, typeof
   172	constexpr uint8_t PATTERN = 0x03;    // 11 - =~, match, regex comparisons
   173	} // namespace Comparison
   174	
   175	// OPERATOR_ASSIGNMENT refinements
   176	namespace Assignment {
   177	constexpr uint8_t SIMPLE = 0x00;      // 00 - =, :=
   178	constexpr uint8_t COMPOUND = 0x01;    // 01 - +=, -=, *=, /=
   179	constexpr uint8_t DESTRUCTURE = 0x02; // 10 - [a,b] = arr, {x,y} = obj
   180	constexpr uint8_t AUGMENTED = 0x03;   // 11 - ||=, &&=, ??=
   181	} // namespace Assignment
   182	
   183	// DEFINITION_CLASS refinements
   184	namespace Class {
   185	constexpr uint8_t REGULAR = 0x00;  // 00 - Basic classes
   186	constexpr uint8_t ABSTRACT = 0x01; // 01 - Abstract classes, interfaces
   187	constexpr uint8_t GENERIC = 0x02;  // 10 - Template/generic classes
   188	constexpr uint8_t ENUM = 0x03;     // 11 - Enums, union types
   189	} // namespace Class
   190	
   191	// FLOW_JUMP refinements
   192	namespace Jump {
   193	constexpr uint8_t RETURN = 0x00;   // 00 - return, yield return
   194	constexpr uint8_t BREAK = 0x01;    // 01 - break, exit loops
   195	constexpr uint8_t CONTINUE = 0x02; // 10 - continue, next, skip
   196	constexpr uint8_t GOTO = 0x03;     // 11 - goto, unconditional jumps
   197	} // namespace Jump
   198	
   199	// NAME_IDENTIFIER refinements
   200	namespace Identifier {
   201	constexpr uint8_t VARIABLE = 0x00; // 00 - Variable references
   202	constexpr uint8_t FUNCTION = 0x01; // 01 - Function name references
   203	constexpr uint8_t TYPE = 0x02;     // 10 - Class/type name references
   204	constexpr uint8_t LABEL = 0x03;    // 11 - Labels, tags
   205	} // namespace Identifier
   206	
   207	// Cross-language query/data patterns (existing, keeping for compatibility)
   208	namespace Query {
   209	constexpr uint8_t SIMPLE = 0x00;   // 00 - Basic queries/comprehensions/selects
   210	constexpr uint8_t NESTED = 0x01;   // 01 - Nested queries/comprehensions/subqueries
   211	constexpr uint8_t FILTERED = 0x02; // 10 - With WHERE/filter clauses
   212	constexpr uint8_t GROUPED = 0x03;  // 11 - With GROUP BY/grouping operations
   213	} // namespace Query
   214	
   215	namespace Aggregation {
   216	constexpr uint8_t SIMPLE = 0x00;      // 00 - Basic reduction (sum, count, reduce)
   217	constexpr uint8_t CONDITIONAL = 0x01; // 01 - Conditional aggregation (filter-then-reduce)
   218	constexpr uint8_t WINDOWED = 0x02;    // 10 - Rolling/windowed operations (sliding window)
   219	constexpr uint8_t GROUPED = 0x03;     // 11 - Group-based aggregation (group by, partition)
   220	} // namespace Aggregation
   221	
   222	namespace Iteration {
   223	constexpr uint8_t MAP = 0x00;    // 00 - Transform operations (map, select, transform)
   224	constexpr uint8_t FILTER = 0x01; // 01 - Filter operations (where, filter, find)
   225	constexpr uint8_t REDUCE = 0x02; // 10 - Reduction operations (fold, reduce, aggregate)
   226	constexpr uint8_t FLAT = 0x03;   // 11 - Flattening operations (flatMap, flatten, SelectMany)
   227	} // namespace Iteration
   228	
   229	namespace Join {
   230	constexpr uint8_t INNER = 0x00; // 00 - Inner joins/intersections/zip
   231	constexpr uint8_t LEFT = 0x01;  // 01 - Left joins/left-biased operations
   232	constexpr uint8_t RIGHT = 0x02; // 10 - Right joins/right-biased operations
   233	constexpr uint8_t OUTER = 0x03; // 11 - Full outer joins/unions/concatenation
   234	} // namespace Join
   235	
   236	// Generic refinement for types that don't need specific refinements
   237	namespace Generic {
   238	constexpr uint8_t UNSPECIFIED = 0x00; // 00 - Default for types without refinements
   239	constexpr uint8_t RESERVED1 = 0x01;   // 01 - Reserved for future use
   240	constexpr uint8_t RESERVED2 = 0x02;   // 10 - Reserved for future use
   241	constexpr uint8_t RESERVED3 = 0x03;   // 11 - Reserved for future use
   242	} // namespace Generic
   243	} // namespace SemanticRefinements
   244	
   245	// Normalized type constants for cross-language consistency

   247	// Declarations/Definitions

   259	constexpr const char *ASSIGNMENT_EXPRESSION = "assignment_expression";
   260	
   261	// Literals
   262	constexpr const char *STRING_LITERAL = "string_literal";
   263	constexpr const char *NUMBER_LITERAL = "number_literal";
   264	constexpr const char *BOOLEAN_LITERAL = "boolean_literal";
   265	constexpr const char *NULL_LITERAL = "null_literal";
   266	
   267	// Control Flow
   268	constexpr const char *IF_STATEMENT = "if_statement";
   269	constexpr const char *FOR_STATEMENT = "for_statement";
   270	constexpr const char *WHILE_STATEMENT = "while_statement";
   271	constexpr const char *RETURN_STATEMENT = "return_statement";
   272	constexpr const char *BREAK_STATEMENT = "break_statement";
   273	constexpr const char *CONTINUE_STATEMENT = "continue_statement";
   274	
   275	// Structure/Organization

   190	
   191	// FLOW_JUMP refinements
   192	namespace Jump {
   193	constexpr uint8_t RETURN = 0x00;   // 00 - return, yield return
   194	constexpr uint8_t BREAK = 0x01;    // 01 - break, exit loops
   195	constexpr uint8_t CONTINUE = 0x02; // 10 - continue, next, skip
   196	constexpr uint8_t GOTO = 0x03;     // 11 - goto, unconditional jumps
   197	} // namespace Jump
   198	
   199	// NAME_IDENTIFIER refinements
   200	namespace Identifier {
   201	constexpr uint8_t VARIABLE = 0x00; // 00 - Variable references
   202	constexpr uint8_t FUNCTION = 0x01; // 01 - Function name references
   203	constexpr uint8_t TYPE = 0x02;     // 10 - Class/type name references
   204	constexpr uint8_t LABEL = 0x03;    // 11 - Labels, tags
   205	} // namespace Identifier
   206	
   207	// Cross-language query/data patterns (existing, keeping for compatibility)
   208	namespace Query {
   209	constexpr uint8_t SIMPLE = 0x00;   // 00 - Basic queries/comprehensions/selects
   210	constexpr uint8_t NESTED = 0x01;   // 01 - Nested queries/comprehensions/subqueries
   211	constexpr uint8_t FILTERED = 0x02; // 10 - With WHERE/filter clauses
   212	constexpr uint8_t GROUPED = 0x03;  // 11 - With GROUP BY/grouping operations
   213	} // namespace Query
   214	
   215	namespace Aggregation {
   216	constexpr uint8_t SIMPLE = 0x00;      // 00 - Basic reduction (sum, count, reduce)
   217	constexpr uint8_t CONDITIONAL = 0x01; // 01 - Conditional aggregation (filter-then-reduce)
   218	constexpr uint8_t WINDOWED = 0x02;    // 10 - Rolling/windowed operations (sliding window)
   219	constexpr uint8_t GROUPED = 0x03;     // 11 - Group-based aggregation (group by, partition)
   220	} // namespace Aggregation
   221	
   222	namespace Iteration {
   223	constexpr uint8_t MAP = 0x00;    // 00 - Transform operations (map, select, transform)
   224	constexpr uint8_t FILTER = 0x01; // 01 - Filter operations (where, filter, find)
   225	constexpr uint8_t REDUCE = 0x02; // 10 - Reduction operations (fold, reduce, aggregate)
   226	constexpr uint8_t FLAT = 0x03;   // 11 - Flattening operations (flatMap, flatten, SelectMany)
   227	} // namespace Iteration
   228	
   229	namespace Join {
   230	constexpr uint8_t INNER = 0x00; // 00 - Inner joins/intersections/zip
   231	constexpr uint8_t LEFT = 0x01;  // 01 - Left joins/left-biased operations
   232	constexpr uint8_t RIGHT = 0x02; // 10 - Right joins/right-biased operations
   233	constexpr uint8_t OUTER = 0x03; // 11 - Full outer joins/unions/concatenation
   234	} // namespace Join
   235	
   236	// Generic refinement for types that don't need specific refinements
   237	namespace Generic {
   238	constexpr uint8_t UNSPECIFIED = 0x00; // 00 - Default for types without refinements
   239	constexpr uint8_t RESERVED1 = 0x01;   // 01 - Reserved for future use
   240	constexpr uint8_t RESERVED2 = 0x02;   // 10 - Reserved for future use
   241	constexpr uint8_t RESERVED3 = 0x03;   // 11 - Reserved for future use
   242	} // namespace Generic
   243	} // namespace SemanticRefinements
   244	
   245	// Normalized type constants for cross-language consistency
   246	namespace NormalizedTypes {
   247	// Declarations/Definitions
   248	constexpr const char *FUNCTION_DECLARATION = "function_declaration";
   249	constexpr const char *CLASS_DECLARATION = "class_declaration";
   250	constexpr const char *VARIABLE_DECLARATION = "variable_declaration";
   251	constexpr const char *METHOD_DECLARATION = "method_declaration";
   252	constexpr const char *PARAMETER_DECLARATION = "parameter_declaration";
   253	
   254	// Expressions/Computations
   255	constexpr const char *FUNCTION_CALL = "function_call";
   256	constexpr const char *VARIABLE_REFERENCE = "variable_reference";
   257	constexpr const char *BINARY_EXPRESSION = "binary_expression";
   258	constexpr const char *UNARY_EXPRESSION = "unary_expression";
   259	constexpr const char *ASSIGNMENT_EXPRESSION = "assignment_expression";
   260	
   261	// Literals
   262	constexpr const char *STRING_LITERAL = "string_literal";
   263	constexpr const char *NUMBER_LITERAL = "number_literal";
   264	constexpr const char *BOOLEAN_LITERAL = "boolean_literal";
   265	constexpr const char *NULL_LITERAL = "null_literal";
   266	
   267	// Control Flow
   268	constexpr const char *IF_STATEMENT = "if_statement";
   269	constexpr const char *FOR_STATEMENT = "for_statement";
   270	constexpr const char *WHILE_STATEMENT = "while_statement";
   271	constexpr const char *RETURN_STATEMENT = "return_statement";
   272	constexpr const char *BREAK_STATEMENT = "break_statement";
   273	constexpr const char *CONTINUE_STATEMENT = "continue_statement";
   274	
   275	// Structure/Organization
   276	constexpr const char *BLOCK = "block";
   277	constexpr const char *MODULE = "module";
   278	constexpr const char *IMPORT_STATEMENT = "import_statement";
   279	constexpr const char *EXPORT_STATEMENT = "export_statement";
   280	
   281	// Other
   282	constexpr const char *COMMENT = "comment";
   283	constexpr const char *IDENTIFIER = "identifier";
   284	constexpr const char *OPERATOR = "operator";
   285	constexpr const char *PUNCTUATION = "punctuation";
   286	} // namespace NormalizedTypes
   287	
   288	} // namespace duckdb

content.content:
{"result":"     1	/**
     2	 * @file python_types.def
     3	 * @brief Python language node type mappings for AST semantic extraction
     4	 *
     5	 * @details This file defines how tree-sitter Python AST nodes are mapped to
     6	 * universal semantic types, along with strategies for extracting names and
     7	 * native context from the parse tree.
     8	 *
     9	 * ## DEF_TYPE Macro Format
    10	 *
    11	 * ```cpp
    12	 * DEF_TYPE(raw_type, semantic_type, name_extraction, native_extraction, flags)
    13	 * ```
    14	 *
    15	 * @param raw_type The tree-sitter node type string (e.g., "function_definition")
    16	 * @param semantic_type Universal semantic classification with optional refinement bits
    17	 * @param name_extraction Strategy for extracting the node's name (see ExtractionStrategy)
    18	 * @param native_extraction Strategy for rich context extraction (see NativeExtractionStrategy)
    19	 * @param flags Behavioral flags (e.g., IS_KEYWORD, IS_SYNTAX_ONLY)
    20	 *
    21	 * ## Semantic Type Encoding
    22	 *
    23	 * Semantic types are 8-bit values where:
    24	 * - Bits 7-2: Base semantic category (e.g., DEFINITION_FUNCTION = 0x04)
    25	 * - Bits 1-0: Refinement within category (e.g., Function::LAMBDA = 0x01)
    26	 *
    27	 * Combined using bitwise OR:
    28	 * ```cpp
    29	 * DEFINITION_FUNCTION | SemanticRefinements::Function::LAMBDA  // = 0x05
    30	 * ```
    31	 *
    32	 * ## Python Language Characteristics
    33	 *
    34	 * Python has several unique features that affect AST mapping:
    35	 *
    36	 * - **Indentation-based blocks**: No explicit braces; tree-sitter uses 'block' nodes
    37	 * - **Decorators**: First-class syntax via `decorated_definition` wrapper nodes
    38	 * - **Comprehensions**: Distinct syntax for list/dict/set comprehensions and generators
    39	 * - **Type hints**: Optional type annotations via `type` and `generic_type` nodes
    40	 * - **Async/await**: Async functions are `function_definition` with `async` keyword child
    41	 * - **Pattern matching**: Python 3.10+ match/case statements
    42	 * - **Walrus operator**: Named expressions with `:=`
    43	 *
    44	 * @see node_config.hpp for ExtractionStrategy and NativeExtractionStrategy definitions
    45	 * @see semantic_types.hpp for full semantic type enumeration
    46	 */
    47	
    48	// =============================================================================
    49	// DEFINITIONS - Functions, Classes, Variables
    50	// =============================================================================
    51	
    52	/**
    53	 * @defgroup python_functions Function Definitions
    54	 * @brief Python function and method definitions
    55	 *
    56	 * Python functions use FIND_IDENTIFIER to locate the function name which is
    57	 * a direct child of `function_definition`. The FUNCTION_WITH_PARAMS native
    58	 * strategy extracts the full signature including parameters and return type.
    59	 *
    60	 * Note: Python does NOT have a distinct `async_function_definition` node in
    61	 * tree-sitter-python. Async functions are regular `function_definition` nodes
    62	 * with an `async` keyword child. The async keyword itself gets FLOW_SYNC.
    63	 * @{
    64	 */
    65	DEF_TYPE("function_definition", DEFINITION_FUNCTION | SemanticRefinements::Function::REGULAR, FIND_IDENTIFIER, FUNCTION_WITH_PARAMS, ASTNodeFlags::IS_CONSTRUCT | ASTNodeFlags::IS_EMBODIED)
    66	DEF_TYPE("async_function_definition", DEFINITION_FUNCTION | SemanticRefinements::Function::ASYNC, FIND_IDENTIFIER, ASYNC_FUNCTION, ASTNodeFlags::IS_CONSTRUCT | ASTNodeFlags::IS_EMBODIED)
    67	/** @} */
    68	
    69	/**
    70	 * @defgroup python_classes Class Definitions
    71	 * @brief Python class definitions including inheritance
    72	 *
    73	 * Classes use CLASS_WITH_METHODS native extraction to capture the class name,
    74	 * base classes, and method signatures. Python classes always have bodies.
    75	 * @{
    76	 */
    77	DEF_TYPE("class_definition", DEFINITION_CLASS, FIND_IDENTIFIER, CLASS_WITH_METHODS, ASTNodeFlags::IS_CONSTRUCT | ASTNodeFlags::IS_EMBODIED)
    78	/** @} */
    79	
    80	/**
    81	 * @defgroup python_variables Variable Definitions
    82	 * @brief Variable assignments and parameter declarations
    83	 *
    84	 * Python variables are always mutable (no const/final keyword). The refinements
    85	 * distinguish between regular assignments (MUTABLE) and function parameters
    86	 * (PARAMETER). Fields are not separately tracked since Python doesn't have
    87	 * formal field declarations.
    88	 * @{
    89	 */
    90	DEF_TYPE("assignment", DEFINITION_VARIABLE | SemanticRefinements::Variable::MUTABLE, FIND_IDENTIFIER, VARIABLE_WITH_TYPE, 0)
    91	DEF_TYPE("typed_parameter", DEFINITION_VARIABLE | SemanticRefinements::Variable::PARAMETER, FIND_IDENTIFIER, NONE, 0)
    92	DEF_TYPE("default_parameter", DEFINITION_VARIABLE | SemanticRefinements::Variable::PARAMETER, FIND_IDENTIFIER, NONE, 0)
    93	DEF_TYPE("typed_default_parameter", DEFINITION_VARIABLE | SemanticRefinements::Variable::PARAMETER, FIND_IDENTIFIER, NONE, 0)
    94	DEF_TYPE("named_expression", DEFINITION_VARIABLE, FIND_IDENTIFIER, NONE, 0)
    95	/** @} */
    96	
    97	// =============================================================================
    98	// COMPUTATIONS - Calls, Operators, Access
    99	// =============================================================================
   100	
   101	/**
   102	 * @defgroup python_calls Function Calls
   103	 * @brief Function and method invocation
   104	 *
   105	 * FIND_CALL_TARGET handles both simple calls (`foo()`) and method calls
   106	 * (`obj.method()`), extracting just the function/method name. FUNCTION_CALL
   107	 * native extraction captures the full call including arguments.
   108	 * @{
   109	 */
   110	DEF_TYPE("call", COMPUTATION_CALL | SemanticRefinements::Call::FUNCTION, FIND_CALL_TARGET, FUNCTION_CALL, 0)
   111	/** @} */
   112	
   113	/**
   114	 * @defgroup python_operators Operators and Expressions
   115	 * @brief Arithmetic, logical, comparison, and assignment operators
   116	 *
   117	 * Python operator nodes include both the compound expression and the
   118	 * individual operator tokens. Compound operators use NONE for name extraction
   119	 * since the operator itself carries the meaning.
   120	 * @{
   121	 */
   122	DEF_TYPE("binary_operator", OPERATOR_ARITHMETIC, NONE, VARIABLE_WITH_TYPE, 0)
   123	DEF_TYPE("unary_operator", OPERATOR_ARITHMETIC, NONE, NONE, 0)
   124	DEF_TYPE("comparison_operator", OPERATOR_COMPARISON, NONE, NONE, 0)
   125	DEF_TYPE("boolean_operator", OPERATOR_LOGICAL, NONE, NONE, 0)
   126	DEF_TYPE("not_operator", OPERATOR_LOGICAL, NONE, NONE, 0)
   127	DEF_TYPE("augmented_assignment", OPERATOR_ASSIGNMENT, NONE, NONE, 0)
   128	/** @} */
   129	
   130	/**
   131	 * @defgroup python_access Attribute and Index Access
   132	 * @brief Dot notation and subscript access patterns
   133	 *
   134	 * Attribute access (`obj.attr`) uses NODE_TEXT to capture the attribute name.
   135	 * Subscript access (`obj[key]`) doesn't have a meaningful name.
   136	 * @{
   137	 */
   138	DEF_TYPE("identifier", NAME_IDENTIFIER, NODE_TEXT, VARIABLE_WITH_TYPE, 0)
   139	DEF_TYPE("attribute", COMPUTATION_ACCESS, NODE_TEXT, VARIABLE_WITH_TYPE, 0)
   140	DEF_TYPE("subscript", COMPUTATION_ACCESS, NONE, NONE, 0)
   141	DEF_TYPE("slice", COMPUTATION_ACCESS, NONE, NONE, 0)
   142	/** @} */
   143	
   144	// =============================================================================
   145	// LITERALS - Strings, Numbers, Collections
   146	// =============================================================================
   147	
   148	/**
   149	 * @defgroup python_literals Literal Values
   150	 * @brief String, numeric, and structured literal values
   151	 *
   152	 * Literals use NODE_TEXT to capture their textual representation. Structured
   153	 * literals (list, dict, set, tuple) use refinements to distinguish container types.
   154	 *
   155	 * Note: String literals in Python are complex due to f-strings, raw strings,
   156	 * and concatenated strings. The tree-sitter grammar breaks these into multiple
   157	 * node types.
   158	 * @{
   159	 */
   160	
   161	// String literals with refinements
   162	DEF_TYPE("string", LITERAL_STRING | SemanticRefinements::String::LITERAL, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   163	DEF_TYPE("string_content", LITERAL_STRING, NONE, NONE, 0)
   164	DEF_TYPE("concatenated_string", LITERAL_STRING, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   165	DEF_TYPE("escape_sequence", LITERAL_STRING, NONE, NONE, 0)
   166	DEF_TYPE("interpolation", LITERAL_STRING, NONE, NONE, 0)
   167	DEF_TYPE("escape_interpolation", LITERAL_STRING, NONE, NONE, 0)
   168	DEF_TYPE("format_specifier", LITERAL_STRING, NONE, NONE, 0)
   169	DEF_TYPE("format_expression", LITERAL_STRING, NONE, NONE, 0)
   170	DEF_TYPE("conversion", LITERAL_STRING, NONE, NONE, 0)
   171	DEF_TYPE("interpolation_conversion", LITERAL_STRING, NONE, NONE, 0)
   172	
   173	// Numeric literals
   174	DEF_TYPE("integer", LITERAL_NUMBER | SemanticRefinements::Number::INTEGER, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   175	DEF_TYPE("float", LITERAL_NUMBER | SemanticRefinements::Number::FLOAT, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   176	
   177	// Atomic literals (singletons)
   178	DEF_TYPE("true", LITERAL_ATOMIC, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   179	DEF_TYPE("false", LITERAL_ATOMIC, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   180	DEF_TYPE("none", LITERAL_ATOMIC, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   181	DEF_TYPE("ellipsis", LITERAL_ATOMIC, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   182	
   183	// Structured literals with container refinements
   184	DEF_TYPE("list", LITERAL_STRUCTURED | SemanticRefinements::Structured::SEQUENCE, NONE, NONE, 0)
   185	DEF_TYPE("tuple", LITERAL_STRUCTURED | SemanticRefinements::Structured::SEQUENCE, NONE, NONE, 0)
   186	DEF_TYPE("dictionary", LITERAL_STRUCTURED | SemanticRefinements::Structured::MAPPING, NONE, NONE, 0)
   187	DEF_TYPE("pair", LITERAL_STRUCTURED | SemanticRefinements::Structured::MAPPING, NONE, NONE, 0)
   188	DEF_TYPE("set", LITERAL_STRUCTURED | SemanticRefinements::Structured::SET, NONE, NONE, 0)
   189	/** @} */
   190	
   191	// =============================================================================
   192	// CONTROL FLOW - Conditionals, Loops, Jumps
   193	// =============================================================================
   194	
   195	/**
   196	 * @defgroup python_control Control Flow
   197	 * @brief Conditional statements, loops, and jump statements
   198	 *
   199	 * Python control flow uses refinements to distinguish between:
   200	 * - Binary conditionals (if/else) vs multiway (match/case)
   201	 * - Iterator loops (for) vs conditional loops (while)
   202	 * - Different jump types (return, break, continue)
   203	 * @{
   204	 */
   205	
   206	// Conditional statements
   207	DEF_TYPE("if_statement", FLOW_CONDITIONAL | SemanticRefinements::Conditional::BINARY, NONE, NONE, 0)
   208	DEF_TYPE("else_clause", FLOW_CONDITIONAL, NONE, NONE, 0)
   209	DEF_TYPE("elif_clause", FLOW_CONDITIONAL, NONE, NONE, 0)
   210	DEF_TYPE("conditional_expression", FLOW_CONDITIONAL | SemanticRefinements::Conditional::TERNARY, NONE, NONE, 0)
   211	DEF_TYPE("match_statement", FLOW_CONDITIONAL | SemanticRefinements::Conditional::MULTIWAY, NONE, NONE, 0)
   212	DEF_TYPE("case_clause", FLOW_CONDITIONAL | SemanticRefinements::Conditional::MULTIWAY, NONE, NONE, 0)
   213	
   214	// Loop statements
   215	DEF_TYPE("for_statement", FLOW_LOOP | SemanticRefinements::Loop::ITERATOR, NONE, NONE, 0)
   216	DEF_TYPE("while_statement", FLOW_LOOP | SemanticRefinements::Loop::CONDITIONAL, NONE, NONE, 0)
   217	DEF_TYPE("for_in_clause", FLOW_LOOP, NONE, NONE, 0)
   218	DEF_TYPE("if_clause", FLOW_CONDITIONAL, NONE, NONE, 0)
   219	
   220	// Jump statements
   221	DEF_TYPE("return_statement", FLOW_JUMP | SemanticRefinements::Jump::RETURN, NONE, VARIABLE_WITH_TYPE, 0)
   222	DEF_TYPE("break_statement", FLOW_JUMP | SemanticRefinements::Jump::BREAK, NONE, NONE, 0)
   223	DEF_TYPE("continue_statement", FLOW_JUMP | SemanticRefinements::Jump::CONTINUE, NONE, NONE, 0)
   224	/** @} */
   225	
   226	// =============================================================================
   227	// ERROR HANDLING - Try/Except/Finally
   228	// =============================================================================
   229	
   230	/**
   231	 * @defgroup python_errors Error Handling
   232	 * @brief Exception handling constructs
   233	 *
   234	 * Python uses try/except/finally for exception handling. The `raise` and
   235	 * `assert` statements both use ERROR_THROW since they can raise exceptions.
   236	 * @{
   237	 */
   238	DEF_TYPE("try_statement", ERROR_TRY, NONE, NONE, 0)
   239	DEF_TYPE("except_clause", ERROR_CATCH, NONE, NONE, 0)
   240	DEF_TYPE("finally_clause", ERROR_FINALLY, NONE, NONE, 0)
   241	DEF_TYPE("raise_statement", ERROR_THROW, NONE, NONE, 0)
   242	DEF_TYPE("assert_statement", ERROR_THROW, NONE, NONE, 0)
   243	/** @} */
   244	
   245	// =============================================================================
   246	// MODULES AND IMPORTS
   247	// =============================================================================
   248	
   249	/**
   250	 * @defgroup python_modules Modules and Imports
   251	 * @brief Module structure and import statements
   252	 *
   253	 * Python modules are the top-level `module` node. Import statements come in
   254	 * several forms:
   255	 * - `import module` (EXTERNAL_IMPORT)
   256	 * - `from module import name` (EXTERNAL_IMPORT)
   257	 * - `from . import name` (relative import)
   258	 * - `from module import *` (wildcard import)
   259	 * @{
   260	 */
   261	DEF_TYPE("module", DEFINITION_MODULE, NONE, NONE, 0)
   262	DEF_TYPE("import_statement", EXTERNAL_IMPORT, NONE, NONE, 0)
   263	DEF_TYPE("import_from_statement", EXTERNAL_IMPORT, NONE, NONE, 0)
   264	DEF_TYPE("aliased_import", EXTERNAL_IMPORT, FIND_IDENTIFIER, NONE, 0)
   265	DEF_TYPE("relative_import", EXTERNAL_IMPORT, NONE, NONE, 0)
   266	DEF_TYPE("import_prefix", EXTERNAL_IMPORT, NONE, NONE, 0)
   267	DEF_TYPE("wildcard_import", EXTERNAL_IMPORT, NONE, NONE, 0)
   268	DEF_TYPE("future_import_statement", EXTERNAL_IMPORT, NONE, NONE, 0)
   269	DEF_TYPE("__future__", EXTERNAL_IMPORT, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   270	/** @} */
   271	
   272	// =============================================================================
   273	// TYPE SYSTEM
   274	// =============================================================================
   275	
   276	/**
   277	 * @defgroup python_types Type Annotations
   278	 * @brief Type hints and generic types (PEP 484+)
   279	 *
   280	 * Python's optional type system uses annotations that don't affect runtime
   281	 * behavior. The `type` node represents a type reference, while `generic_type`
   282	 * handles parameterized types like `List[int]` or `Dict[str, Any]`.
   283	 * @{
   284	 */
   285	DEF_TYPE("type", TYPE_REFERENCE, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   286	DEF_TYPE("generic_type", TYPE_GENERIC, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   287	DEF_TYPE("type_parameter", TYPE_GENERIC, FIND_IDENTIFIER, NONE, 0)
   288	DEF_TYPE("->", TYPE_REFERENCE, NONE, NONE, 0)
   289	DEF_TYPE("type_conversion", TYPE_REFERENCE, NONE, NONE, 0)
   290	/** @} */
   291	
   292	// =============================================================================
   293	// COMPREHENSIONS AND GENERATORS
   294	// =============================================================================
   295	
   296	/**
   297	 * @defgroup python_comprehensions Comprehensions and Generators
   298	 * @brief List/dict/set comprehensions and generator expressions
   299	 *
   300	 * Python comprehensions are a distinct syntax for creating collections via
   301	 * iteration. They map to TRANSFORM_QUERY with refinements:
   302	 * - SIMPLE: List/dict/set comprehensions `[x for x in items]`
   303	 * - NESTED: Generator expressions `(x for x in items)`
   304	 * @{
   305	 */
   306	DEF_TYPE("list_comprehension", TRANSFORM_QUERY | SemanticRefinements::Query::SIMPLE, NONE, NONE, 0)
   307	DEF_TYPE("dictionary_comprehension", TRANSFORM_QUERY | SemanticRefinements::Query::SIMPLE, NONE, NONE, 0)
   308	DEF_TYPE("set_comprehension", TRANSFORM_QUERY | SemanticRefinements::Query::SIMPLE, NONE, NONE, 0)
   309	DEF_TYPE("generator_expression", TRANSFORM_QUERY | SemanticRefinements::Query::NESTED, NONE, NONE, 0)
   310	/** @} */
   311	
   312	// =============================================================================
   313	// DECORATORS AND ANNOTATIONS
   314	// =============================================================================
   315	
   316	/**
   317	 * @defgroup python_decorators Decorators and Metadata
   318	 * @brief Decorator syntax and annotation patterns
   319	 *
   320	 * Decorators are a form of metaprogramming that wrap functions/classes.
   321	 * The `decorated_definition` node wraps the actual definition and contains
   322	 * one or more `decorator` children.
   323	 * @{
   324	 */
   325	DEF_TYPE("decorator", NAME_ATTRIBUTE, FIND_IDENTIFIER, NONE, 0)
   326	DEF_TYPE("decorated_definition", METADATA_ANNOTATION, NONE, NONE, 0)
   327	DEF_TYPE("as_clause", METADATA_ANNOTATION, FIND_IDENTIFIER, NONE, 0)
   328	DEF_TYPE("global_statement", METADATA_ANNOTATION, NONE, NONE, 0)
   329	DEF_TYPE("nonlocal_statement", METADATA_ANNOTATION, NONE, NONE, 0)
   330	/** @} */
   331	
   332	// =============================================================================
   333	// PATTERN MATCHING (Python 3.10+)
   334	// =============================================================================
   335	
   336	/**
   337	 * @defgroup python_patterns Pattern Matching
   338	 * @brief Structural pattern matching constructs (PEP 634)
   339	 *
   340	 * Python 3.10 introduced match/case statements with various pattern types:
   341	 * - PATTERN_DESTRUCTURE: Extracts values from structures
   342	 * - PATTERN_MATCH: Matches against specific patterns

   344	 * @{
   345	 */
   346	DEF_TYPE("case_pattern", PATTERN_MATCH, NONE, NONE, 0)

   364	
   365	// =============================================================================
   366	// SCOPED REFERENCES
   367	// =============================================================================
   368	
   369	/**
   370	 * @defgroup python_scoped Scoped References
   371	 * @brief Self, super, and cls references
   372	 *
   373	 * Python uses `self` for instance references and `cls` for class references
   374	 * by convention. `super()` provides parent class access.

   377	DEF_TYPE("self", NAME_SCOPED, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)

   451	/**

   470	DEF_TYPE("for", FLOW_LOOP, NONE, NONE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_KEYWORD)
   471	DEF_TYPE("while", FLOW_LOOP, NONE, NONE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_KEYWORD)
   472	DEF_TYPE("match", FLOW_CONDITIONAL | SemanticRefinements::Conditional::MULTIWAY, NONE, NONE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_KEYWORD)
   473	DEF_TYPE("case", FLOW_CONDITIONAL | SemanticRefinements::Conditional::MULTIWAY, NONE, NONE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_KEYWORD)
   474	
   475	// Jump keywords

   477	DEF_TYPE("break", FLOW_JUMP, NONE, NONE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_KEYWORD)
   478	DEF_TYPE("continue", FLOW_JUMP, NONE, NONE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_KEYWORD)
   479	DEF_TYPE("pass", EXECUTION_STATEMENT, NONE, NONE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_KEYWORD)
   480	
   481	// Async keywords
   482	DEF_TYPE("async", FLOW_SYNC, NONE, NONE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_KEYWORD)
   483	DEF_TYPE("await", FLOW_SYNC, NONE, NONE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_KEYWORD)
   484	DEF_TYPE("yield", FLOW_SYNC, NONE, NONE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_KEYWORD)
   485	DEF_TYPE("with", FLOW_SYNC, NONE, NONE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_KEYWORD)
   486	
   487	// Import keywords
   488	DEF_TYPE("import", EXTERNAL_IMPORT, NONE, NONE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_KEYWORD)
   489	DEF_TYPE("from", EXTERNAL_IMPORT, NONE, NONE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_KEYWORD)
   490	
   491	// Error handling keywords
   492	DEF_TYPE("try", ERROR_TRY, NONE, NONE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_KEYWORD)
   493	DEF_TYPE("except", ERROR_CATCH, NONE, NONE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_KEYWORD)
   494	DEF_TYPE("finally", ERROR_FINALLY, NONE, NONE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_KEYWORD)
   495	DEF_TYPE("raise", ERROR_THROW, NONE, NONE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_KEYWORD)
   496	DEF_TYPE("assert", ERROR_THROW, NONE, NONE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_KEYWORD)
   497	
   498	// Other keywords
   499	DEF_TYPE("as", METADATA_ANNOTATION, NONE, NONE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_KEYWORD)
   500	DEF_TYPE("global", METADATA_ANNOTATION, NONE, NONE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_KEYWORD)
   501	DEF_TYPE("nonlocal", METADATA_ANNOTATION, NONE, NONE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_KEYWORD)
   502	DEF_TYPE("del", EXECUTION_MUTATION, NONE, NONE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_KEYWORD)
   503	DEF_TYPE("print", EXECUTION_STATEMENT_CALL, NONE, NONE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_KEYWORD)
   504	DEF_TYPE("exec", EXECUTION_STATEMENT_CALL, NONE, NONE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_KEYWORD)
   505	/** @} */
   506	
   507	// =============================================================================
   508	// OPERATORS
   509	// =============================================================================
   510	
   511	/**
   512	 * @defgroup python_operators_tokens Operator Tokens
   513	 * @brief Individual operator symbols
   514	 * @{
   515	 */
   516	
   517	// Arithmetic operators
   518	DEF_TYPE("+", OPERATOR_ARITHMETIC, NONE, NONE, 0)
   519	DEF_TYPE("-", OPERATOR_ARITHMETIC, NONE, NONE, 0)
   520	DEF_TYPE("*", OPERATOR_ARITHMETIC, NONE, NONE, 0)

   531	DEF_TYPE(">>", OPERATOR_ARITHMETIC, NONE, NONE, 0)
   532	
   533	// Logical operators
   534	DEF_TYPE("and", OPERATOR_LOGICAL, NONE, NONE, 0)
   535	DEF_TYPE("or", OPERATOR_LOGICAL, NONE, NONE, 0)
   536	DEF_TYPE("not", OPERATOR_LOGICAL, NONE, NONE, 0)
   537	
   538	// Comparison operators
   539	DEF_TYPE("==", OPERATOR_COMPARISON, NONE, NONE, 0)
   540	DEF_TYPE("!=", OPERATOR_COMPARISON, NONE, NONE, 0)
   541	DEF_TYPE("<", OPERATOR_COMPARISON, NONE, NONE, 0)
   542	DEF_TYPE(">", OPERATOR_COMPARISON, NONE, NONE, 0)
   543	DEF_TYPE("<=", OPERATOR_COMPARISON, NONE, NONE, 0)
   544	DEF_TYPE(">=", OPERATOR_COMPARISON, NONE, NONE, 0)
   545	DEF_TYPE("<>", OPERATOR_COMPARISON, NONE, NONE, 0)

   550	
   551	// Assignment operators
   552	DEF_TYPE("=", OPERATOR_ASSIGNMENT, NONE, NONE, 0)

{"result":"     1	/**
     2	 * @file python_types.def
     3	 * @brief Python language node type mappings for AST semantic extraction
     4	 *
     5	 * @details This file defines how tree-sitter Python AST nodes are mapped to
     6	 * universal semantic types, along with strategies for extracting names and
     7	 * native context from the parse tree.
     8	 *
     9	 * ## DEF_TYPE Macro Format
    10	 *
    11	 * ```cpp
    12	 * DEF_TYPE(raw_type, semantic_type, name_extraction, native_extraction, flags)
    13	 * ```
    14	 *
    15	 * @param raw_type The tree-sitter node type string (e.g., "function_definition")
    16	 * @param semantic_type Universal semantic classification with optional refinement bits
    17	 * @param name_extraction Strategy for extracting the node's name (see ExtractionStrategy)
    18	 * @param native_extraction Strategy for rich context extraction (see NativeExtractionStrategy)
    19	 * @param flags Behavioral flags (e.g., IS_KEYWORD, IS_SYNTAX_ONLY)
    20	 *
    21	 * ## Semantic Type Encoding
    22	 *
    23	 * Semantic types are 8-bit values where:
    24	 * - Bits 7-2: Base semantic category (e.g., DEFINITION_FUNCTION = 0x04)
    25	 * - Bits 1-0: Refinement within category (e.g., Function::LAMBDA = 0x01)
    26	 *
    27	 * Combined using bitwise OR:
    28	 * ```cpp
    29	 * DEFINITION_FUNCTION | SemanticRefinements::Function::LAMBDA  // = 0x05
    30	 * ```
    31	 *
    32	 * ## Python Language Characteristics
    33	 *
    34	 * Python has several unique features that affect AST mapping:
    35	 *
    36	 * - **Indentation-based blocks**: No explicit braces; tree-sitter uses 'block' nodes
    37	 * - **Decorators**: First-class syntax via `decorated_definition` wrapper nodes
    38	 * - **Comprehensions**: Distinct syntax for list/dict/set comprehensions and generators
    39	 * - **Type hints**: Optional type annotations via `type` and `generic_type` nodes
    40	 * - **Async/await**: Async functions are `function_definition` with `async` keyword child
    41	 * - **Pattern matching**: Python 3.10+ match/case statements
    42	 * - **Walrus operator**: Named expressions with `:=`
    43	 *
    44	 * @see node_config.hpp for ExtractionStrategy and NativeExtractionStrategy definitions
    45	 * @see semantic_types.hpp for full semantic type enumeration
    46	 */
    47	
    48	// =============================================================================
    49	// DEFINITIONS - Functions, Classes, Variables
    50	// =============================================================================
    51	
    52	/**
    53	 * @defgroup python_functions Function Definitions
    54	 * @brief Python function and method definitions
    55	 *
    56	 * Python functions use FIND_IDENTIFIER to locate the function name which is
    57	 * a direct child of `function_definition`. The FUNCTION_WITH_PARAMS native
    58	 * strategy extracts the full signature including parameters and return type.
    59	 *
    60	 * Note: Python does NOT have a distinct `async_function_definition` node in
    61	 * tree-sitter-python. Async functions are regular `function_definition` nodes
    62	 * with an `async` keyword child. The async keyword itself gets FLOW_SYNC.
    63	 * @{
    64	 */
    65	DEF_TYPE("function_definition", DEFINITION_FUNCTION | SemanticRefinements::Function::REGULAR, FIND_IDENTIFIER, FUNCTION_WITH_PARAMS, ASTNodeFlags::IS_CONSTRUCT | ASTNodeFlags::IS_EMBODIED)
    66	DEF_TYPE("async_function_definition", DEFINITION_FUNCTION | SemanticRefinements::Function::ASYNC, FIND_IDENTIFIER, ASYNC_FUNCTION, ASTNodeFlags::IS_CONSTRUCT | ASTNodeFlags::IS_EMBODIED)
    67	/** @} */
    68	
    69	/**
    70	 * @defgroup python_classes Class Definitions
    71	 * @brief Python class definitions including inheritance
    72	 *
    73	 * Classes use CLASS_WITH_METHODS native extraction to capture the class name,
    74	 * base classes, and method signatures. Python classes always have bodies.
    75	 * @{
    76	 */
    77	DEF_TYPE("class_definition", DEFINITION_CLASS, FIND_IDENTIFIER, CLASS_WITH_METHODS, ASTNodeFlags::IS_CONSTRUCT | ASTNodeFlags::IS_EMBODIED)
    78	/** @} */
    79	
    80	/**
    81	 * @defgroup python_variables Variable Definitions
    82	 * @brief Variable assignments and parameter declarations
    83	 *
    84	 * Python variables are always mutable (no const/final keyword). The refinements
    85	 * distinguish between regular assignments (MUTABLE) and function parameters
    86	 * (PARAMETER). Fields are not separately tracked since Python doesn't have
    87	 * formal field declarations.
    88	 * @{
    89	 */
    90	DEF_TYPE("assignment", DEFINITION_VARIABLE | SemanticRefinements::Variable::MUTABLE, FIND_IDENTIFIER, VARIABLE_WITH_TYPE, 0)
    91	DEF_TYPE("typed_parameter", DEFINITION_VARIABLE | SemanticRefinements::Variable::PARAMETER, FIND_IDENTIFIER, NONE, 0)
    92	DEF_TYPE("default_parameter", DEFINITION_VARIABLE | SemanticRefinements::Variable::PARAMETER, FIND_IDENTIFIER, NONE, 0)
    93	DEF_TYPE("typed_default_parameter", DEFINITION_VARIABLE | SemanticRefinements::Variable::PARAMETER, FIND_IDENTIFIER, NONE, 0)
    94	DEF_TYPE("named_expression", DEFINITION_VARIABLE, FIND_IDENTIFIER, NONE, 0)
    95	/** @} */
    96	
    97	// =============================================================================
    98	// COMPUTATIONS - Calls, Operators, Access
    99	// =============================================================================
   100	
   101	/**
   102	 * @defgroup python_calls Function Calls
   103	 * @brief Function and method invocation
   104	 *
   105	 * FIND_CALL_TARGET handles both simple calls (`foo()`) and method calls
   106	 * (`obj.method()`), extracting just the function/method name. FUNCTION_CALL
   107	 * native extraction captures the full call including arguments.
   108	 * @{
   109	 */
   110	DEF_TYPE("call", COMPUTATION_CALL | SemanticRefinements::Call::FUNCTION, FIND_CALL_TARGET, FUNCTION_CALL, 0)
   111	/** @} */
   112	
   113	/**
   114	 * @defgroup python_operators Operators and Expressions
   115	 * @brief Arithmetic, logical, comparison, and assignment operators
   116	 *
   117	 * Python operator nodes include both the compound expression and the
   118	 * individual operator tokens. Compound operators use NONE for name extraction
   119	 * since the operator itself carries the meaning.
   120	 * @{
   121	 */
   122	DEF_TYPE("binary_operator", OPERATOR_ARITHMETIC, NONE, VARIABLE_WITH_TYPE, 0)
   123	DEF_TYPE("unary_operator", OPERATOR_ARITHMETIC, NONE, NONE, 0)
   124	DEF_TYPE("comparison_operator", OPERATOR_COMPARISON, NONE, NONE, 0)
   125	DEF_TYPE("boolean_operator", OPERATOR_LOGICAL, NONE, NONE, 0)
   126	DEF_TYPE("not_operator", OPERATOR_LOGICAL, NONE, NONE, 0)
   127	DEF_TYPE("augmented_assignment", OPERATOR_ASSIGNMENT, NONE, NONE, 0)

   566	DEF_TYPE(":=", OPERATOR_ASSIGNMENT, NONE, NONE, 0)
   567	/** @} */
   568	
   569	// =============================================================================
   570	// PUNCTUATION AND DELIMITERS
   571	// =============================================================================
   572	
   573	/**
   574	 * @defgroup python_punctuation Punctuation
   575	 * @brief Delimiters, separators, and syntax markers
   576	 * @{
   577	 */
   578	DEF_TYPE("(", PARSER_DELIMITER, NONE, NONE, 0)
   579	DEF_TYPE(")", PARSER_DELIMITER, NONE, NONE, 0)
   580	DEF_TYPE("[", PARSER_DELIMITER, NONE, NONE, 0)
   581	DEF_TYPE("]", PARSER_DELIMITER, NONE, NONE, 0)
   582	DEF_TYPE("{", PARSER_DELIMITER, NONE, NONE, 0)
   583	DEF_TYPE("}", PARSER_DELIMITER, NONE, NONE, 0)
   584	DEF_TYPE(",", PARSER_PUNCTUATION, NONE, NONE, 0)
   585	DEF_TYPE(":", PARSER_PUNCTUATION, NONE, NONE, 0)
   586	DEF_TYPE(";", PARSER_PUNCTUATION, NONE, NONE, 0)
   587	DEF_TYPE(".", PARSER_PUNCTUATION, NONE, NONE, 0)
   588	DEF_TYPE("string_start", PARSER_PUNCTUATION, NONE, NONE, 0)
   589	DEF_TYPE("string_end", PARSER_PUNCTUATION, NONE, NONE, 0)
   590	DEF_TYPE("keyword_separator", PARSER_PUNCTUATION, NONE, NONE, 0)
   591	DEF_TYPE("chevron", PARSER_PUNCTUATION, NONE, NONE, 0)
   592	DEF_TYPE("positional_separator", PARSER_PUNCTUATION, NONE, NONE, 0)
   593	DEF_TYPE("line_continuation", PARSER_SYNTAX, NONE, NONE, 0)
   594	/** @} */
   595	
   596	// =============================================================================
   597	// ERROR RECOVERY
   598	// =============================================================================
   599	
   600	/**
   601	 * @defgroup python_errors_parse Parse Errors
   602	 * @brief Error nodes from failed parsing
   603	 * @{
   604	 */
   605	DEF_TYPE("ERROR", PARSER_SYNTAX, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   606	/** @} */
"}
